Cats don't have a collarbone, so they can fit through any opening the size of their head.
Cats groom themselves not just to stay clean, but also to regulate their body temperature.
A cat has 32 muscles in each ear and can rotate them 180 degrees.
Cats spend an average of 70% of their day sleeping.
Cats meow only to communicate with humans; they hiss or purr to talk to other cats.
A cat's nose print is unique, much like a human fingerprint.
Domestic cats can reach speeds of up to 30 mph (48 km/h).
Cats cannot taste sweetness.
A cat can jump up to 6 times its own height.
Cats see 6 times better in the dark than humans can.
Cats have 5 toes on their front paws and 4 on their back paws.
Cats may purr when they are stressed or in pain, not just when they're happy.
There are estimated to be over 500 million domestic cats in the world.
Most cats dislike water because their fur gets extremely heavy when wet.
In Ancient Egypt, cats were considered sacred, and killing one was a capital crime.
A cat's whiskers are generally as wide as its body, helping it navigate tight spaces.
A cat's heart beats 110 to 140 times per minute, twice as fast as a human's.
Cats walk on their toes.
The longest cat ever recorded was 48.5 inches (123 cm) long.
Cats can make about 100 different sounds, whereas dogs can only make about 10.
